Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is a major urban center in Nigeria, known for its economic growth and cultural diversity. It is one of the most important cities in the country, serving as a hub for business, education, and recreation. The city is home to several universities and institutions of higher learning, contributing to its vibrant academic environment.

Sec. 2  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is located in the state of Lagos, Nigeria, and is part of the Lagos State government. It is strategically positioned along the Lagos-Ibadan highway, making it a key transportation hub. The city is also known for its infrastructure development, including roads, bridges, and public transportation systems.

Sec. 3  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is a rapidly growing city, with a population that continues to increase. It has experienced significant urbanization and development over the years, with new residential areas, commercial centers, and public facilities being constructed. The city is also known for its modern architecture and urban planning.

Sec. 4  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is a major economic center in Nigeria, with a wide range of industries and businesses operating within the city. It is home to several industrial zones, manufacturing plants, and commercial enterprises. The city is also a key player in the Nigerian economy, contributing to national development and employment opportunities.

Sec. 5  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is a culturally rich city, with a diverse population that includes people from various ethnic backgrounds. It is known for its festivals, traditional music, and arts.
